---
pagetitle: "Philip Felix — Senior Data Scientist"
body-classes: home-page
toc: false
title-block-style: none
---

::: {.hero-section}

# Philip Felix

::: {.hero-subtitle}
Senior Data Scientist — SF Bay Area
:::

::: {.hero-bio}
A decade of data science work with marketing, sales, and product teams — most recently at Atlassian, before that Square. Currently focused on where LLMs make GTM analytics sharper: context-aware agents, rigorous evals, and measurement work where standard attribution breaks. [More about me →](about.qmd)
:::

:::

::: {.section-heading}
Projects
:::

::: {.project-grid}

::: {.project-card}
::: {.project-card-title}
[Marketing Measurement Under Hypergrowth](projects/marketing-measurement.qmd)
:::
::: {.project-card-description}
When a company grows from $1B to $30B in 15 months on mostly organic adoption, every standard measurement approach breaks. An interactive app showing why attribution overcredits by 3-9x and how to build a measurement stack that works.
:::
::: {.project-card-stack}
Python · Next.js · TypeScript · Visx · Recharts
:::
:::

::: {.project-card}
::: {.project-card-title}
[Support Ticket Classification](projects/support-ticket-classification.qmd)
:::
::: {.project-card-description}
Which classification method should you use — and how does the answer change based on how much labeled data you have? Seven methods compared from 100 to ~9.5K labeled tickets, from TF-IDF baselines to fine-tuned LLMs, on synthetic data modeled after a real AI-product support taxonomy.
:::
::: {.project-card-stack}
Python · Next.js · TypeScript · Recharts
:::
:::

::: {.project-card}
::: {.project-card-title}
[Adaptive Analyst Agent](projects/context-agent.qmd)
:::
::: {.project-card-description}
An LLM-powered analyst that answers the same business question differently depending on who's asking. A VP gets a board-level summary. A campaign manager gets channel attribution and budget recommendations. Same data, different context.
:::
::: {.project-card-stack}
Python · Claude API · DuckDB · Streamlit
:::
:::

::: {.project-card}
::: {.project-card-title}
[Lead Scoring & Buying Group Identification](projects/lead-scoring.qmd)
:::
::: {.project-card-description}
You have 100K accounts and can only engage a few thousand this quarter. Which ones? A propensity model to answer that, plus a buying group analyzer that figures out why good accounts aren't closing and who's missing from the deal.
:::
::: {.project-card-stack}
Python · Pandas · scikit-learn · Streamlit
:::
:::

::: {.project-card}
::: {.project-card-title}
[Job Title Normalizer](projects/title-normalizer.qmd)
:::
::: {.project-card-description}
Sales and marketing teams route deals and score leads off job titles, but CRM titles are chaos — abbreviations, regions, seniority markers, punctuation. A multi-task PyTorch classifier that normalizes them into clean seniority and function labels.
:::
::: {.project-card-stack}
Python · PyTorch · scikit-learn
:::
:::

::: {.project-card}
::: {.project-card-title}
[InterviewReady](projects/interview-ready.qmd)
:::
::: {.project-card-description}
Paste a job description, get a tailored practice session — technical, case, or behavioral questions calibrated to the role and company. Answers are evaluated in-app by Claude with scoring and coaching. Skill-readiness tracking across sessions.
:::
::: {.project-card-stack}
Next.js · TypeScript · Supabase · Claude API
:::
:::

::: {.project-card}
::: {.project-card-title}
[AI Adoption Playbook](projects/ai-adoption-playbook.qmd)
:::
::: {.project-card-description}
A practical framework for getting a data science team from "what's an MCP server?" to LLM-assisted workflows. The actual steps, in order, with common blockers and what to do about them.
:::
::: {.project-card-stack}
Claude · MCP · Cursor · Databricks CLI
:::
:::

:::

::: {.contact-section}
Looking for senior data science roles where the work is collaborative and the problems are hard. If you're hiring — or just want to trade notes on LLM evals, GTM analytics, or how to get a data team actually using AI — [pfelix828@gmail.com](mailto:pfelix828@gmail.com).

::: {.contact-links}
[GitHub](https://github.com/pfelix828) · [LinkedIn](https://linkedin.com/in/pfelix1)
:::
:::
